Title: GraphQL Error Handling - Invalid Query
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI accessible at {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql; no auth required for this test
Test_Data: malformedQuery="{ products { INVALID_FIELD } }"; missingArgsQuery="mutation { addItem }"; nonExistentField="{ nonExistentTopLevelField }"
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type malformed query: { products { INVALID_FIELD } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — verify structured GraphQL error returned (not crash)
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query for non-existent field: { nonExistentTopLevelField }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — verify field-level error message
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type mutation with missing required args: mutation { addItem }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — verify argument validation error
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows error data (red text or errors block — not blank/crash)
[ERRORS] Malformed syntax: errors[] non-empty with message describing syntax error
[ERRORS] Unknown field: errors[].message references the unknown field by name
[ERRORS] Missing args: errors[].message references missing argument name
[DATA] All error responses follow GraphQL error format: { errors: [{ message, locations, path }] }
[DATA] No stack traces or internal paths in error messages
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No unhandled server exceptions in GraphiQL console
[RESPONSE] GraphiQL remains functional after each error (not frozen/crashed)
Failure_Signals: GraphiQL crashes or freezes; stack trace in errors[].message; no errors[] on obviously invalid query; blank response panel

Title: GraphQL Authentication - Unauthenticated Access
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI accessible at {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql; products exist; auth tokens available
Test_Data: publicQuery (no token); protectedQuery (orders, no token); mutationNoToken (addItem without auth); validToken={{USER_TOKEN}}
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Click "Headers" button (right tab in bottom panel — NOT "Variables" which is the left/default tab) → click inside the Headers editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace) → leave empty (no auth)
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type public query: query { products(storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}") { totalCount items { id name } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — verify public query succeeds without auth
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type protected query: query { orders { totalCount } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — verify authentication error returned
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type mutation: mutation { addItem(command: { storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" currencyCode: "USD" productId: "{{TEST_SKU}}" quantity: 1 }) { id itemsCount } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — verify authentication error returned
[ACT] Click "Headers" button (right tab in bottom panel — NOT "Variables" which is the left/default tab) → click inside the Headers editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace) → type: {"Authorization": "Bearer {{USER_TOKEN}}"} → verify the token appears under the Headers region (not Variables)
[ACT] Click query editor → clear → type orders query again
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — verify authenticated query succeeds
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Public products query: data.products returned; no errors[]
[ERRORS] Orders query without auth: errors[] non-empty with UNAUTHORIZED message (BL-GQL-004)
[ERRORS] addItem without auth: errors[] non-empty with UNAUTHORIZED message
[DATA] Error messages contain no user IDs, internal paths, or password hints
[DATA] errors[].extensions.code = "UNAUTHORIZED" or equivalent
[DATA] Authenticated orders query succeeds after setting token
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No server errors in GraphiQL
[RESPONSE] GraphiQL remains functional throughout auth/no-auth switching
Failure_Signals: data returned for orders/mutations without auth; GraphiQL shows error banner on public query; error message exposes internal details; authenticated query fails with valid token

Title: GraphQL Query Performance
Test_Data: GraphiQL UI loaded; products and orders available; auth header set
Steps: product search query; complex nested product query (variants + prices + images); orders list query
Assertions:
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type products search query → Execute → note response time in GraphiQL or DevTools Network
[ACT] Clear editor → type complex nested product query with variants, prices, images → Execute → note response time
[ACT] Clear editor → type orders list query → Execute → note response time
[ACT] Re-execute nested query 5 times (Ctrl+Enter each); check response times do not grow proportionally (N+1 detection)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[DATA] Product search response time < 500ms
[DATA] Nested product query response time < 1000ms
[DATA] Orders list response time < 500ms
[DATA] Repeated nested queries: response times remain within 20% of first call (no N+1 pattern)
[ERRORS] No errors[] in any response
Failure_Signals: [CONSOLE] No timeout errors or slow query warnings in GraphiQL
Cleanup: Response time exceeds threshold; response times grow linearly with nesting depth (N+1); errors[] on any query; GraphiQL shows timeout

Title: GraphQL Introspection Security
Preconditions: GraphiQL UI accessible at {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql; no auth required for introspection test
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql
[WAIT] GraphiQL editor loaded
[ACT] Click query editor → clear (Ctrl+A, Backspace)
[ACT] Type query: { __schema { types { name } } }
[GQL] Click Execute (▶) or Ctrl+Enter
[READ] Read response — check introspection enabled/disabled status
[ACT] If enabled: scan type names in response for sensitive internal types
[ACT] Re-execute introspection query 10 times rapidly (Ctrl+Enter) to test rate limiting
Assertions:
[RESPONSE] Response panel shows data or structured error (not crash)
[DATA] If introspection disabled: errors[] contains message indicating introspection not allowed (acceptable for production)
[DATA] If introspection enabled: __schema.types returned; no types with names containing "internal" "debug" "admin" "secret"
[DATA] Rate limiting applies (10 rapid queries; responses throttled after threshold)
[DATA] Response is valid JSON in either case
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No server errors in GraphiQL
[RESPONSE] GraphiQL Docs sidebar (📖) reflects schema availability
Failure_Signals: GraphiQL crashes on introspection; internal type names exposed in schema; no rate limiting on repeated introspection; server errors in console
